content.text:
The implementation currently hardcodes `SWORD_BASE_PREMIUM = 100` for any non-empty item list. So calling `quote` with an amulet won't be a compilation error - the function exists and takes the right shape. It will be a runtime error: returning 115 instead of 71.

Since the function already exists and takes any item shape (Item type accepts `type: string`), there will be no compilation error. Let me reconsider - the typical TDD two-stage flow assumes the function doesn't exist yet. In this case the function exists but produces wrong output, so we go straight to runtime error.

Let me make my prediction and run the test.

Red Phase - Compilation Error Prediction:
- Test: "newcomer single amulet yields premium 71 G (60 base + 6 first-insurance + 5 fee)"
- Expected: No compilation error (function `quote` already exists and `Item.type` accepts any string)
- Reason: Function signature already accepts amulet items; this test will skip directly to runtime error

Red Phase - Runtime Error Prediction:
- Test: "newcomer single amulet yields premium 71 G"
- Expected: Runtime assertion error
- Expected value: 71
- Actual value: 115 (current code uses SWORD_BASE_PREMIUM=100 for any non-empty item list: 100 + 10 + 5 = 115)
- Diff:
  Expected: 71
  Received: 115

Now let me run the test to verify.
